pattern: Enumerate all six ways to split a grid into three non-overlapping axis-aligned rectangles using two cuts; for each partition compute bounding-box areas and take the global minimum.
hints: There are only three cut patterns: two horizontal cuts, two vertical cuts, or one horizontal plus one vertical cut. For the mixed cut, two sub-patterns exist (which side is the full-width band), giving six configurations total.
For each split, the optimal rectangle for a sub-region is the tightest bounding box around all 1s in that region — precompute or compute on the fly.
Use the constraint that the grid is at most 30x30 to allow an O(R^2 * C^2) enumeration of all split pairs.

edge_inputs.description: three ones in a single row — three unit rectangles
three ones in a single column
ones at three corners of a 3x3 grid
all ones dense block — forced to split into overlapping bounding boxes
three clustered regions — one per split band is optimal

stress.description: random 15x15 binary grids with between 3 and 10 ones
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    R = C = max(3, size)
    grid = [[0]*C for _ in range(R)]
    positions = [(i,j) for i in range(R) for j in range(C)]
    chosen = rng.sample(positions, rng.randint(3, min(10, R*C)))
    for i,j in chosen:
        grid[i][j] = 1
    return [grid]
